Unity can also be activated by acting together with others in coordinated or synchronized ways. We humans have a long history of this kind of behavior, especially within our we groups. In fact, unity created through acting together can be strong enough to move people to cooperate with and even to agree more often with the requests of their co-actors. So let's say you're in a position of assigning tasks within your work team. If you want to elevate feelings of unity among team members, rather than assigning them sub-tasks to be done individually, have them work collectively or in small groups in coordinated and synchronized ways. The effects on collaboration, helpfulness, and group solidarity should be notable.
